title: Dice Roll Simulation
hints: Think about building the sequence one roll at a time. What information from the previous rolls determines whether the next roll is valid?
Use dynamic programming where the state tracks the current length, the last value rolled, and how many times it has appeared consecutively so far.
The answer is the sum of all valid states at length n. A state is invalid if the consecutive count exceeds the limit for that face. Start with length 0 having count 0 for any face.
